pulp-view-embed.name: Pulp View Embed
pulp-view-embed.description: Flat C ABI for embedding a Pulp-imported frontend inside a foreign C++ host (JUCE, iPlug2, bespoke) without linking Pulp's C++ ABI

macOS.notes: Source builds require an installed Pulp SDK discoverable via find_package(Pulp CONFIG REQUIRED) (set CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH). Prebuilt distributions ship a pulp_view_embedConfig.cmake so a foreign host can find_package(pulp_view_embed) without any Pulp build.

Linux.notes: Verified 2026-06-08 (X11). Same find_package(Pulp) flow as macOS; the installed Pulp SDK re-finds ICU and exposes pulp_link_fontconfig_after_skia() — call it on each Skia-linking exe (system ICU + fontconfig dev libs required). Headless render + live X11 host (Dawn Vulkan surface from the X11 window) both proven. A relocatable Linux SDK tarball is produced by tools/package-sdk.sh.

pulp-view-embed.unique_value: Lets a non-Pulp C/C++ host embed a Pulp-imported frontend (e.g. a Figma design) as a rendered child view through a stable C ABI, with a bidirectional host<->view parameter bridge and an offscreen/texture render mode.
